other_observers: None; David Silverman who found the bird
specific_location: North end of Rye Mountain Park just east of Preseren Rd. and north of Picnic Area Sign & Rd.
description: Bird was about same size as Lazuli/Indigo Buntings. Overall color was dull greenish olive with greener back, wing covert & secondary linings. Primaries were dark olive. Underparts were a dull light olive. The dull colors of this bird suggests a 1st year bird as adult females are bright green.  Bill was thick, finchlike & conical, darker at tip and a lighter flesh color at base. Eye was dark.  The most outstanding feature was the bird's lack of wingbars, streaks, and lack of any mottling which are present on most finches, buntings, and sparrows.Behaviors: Bird flew into a low perch about a foot from ground, gave observer a brief look, and flew off. It has not been refound as of this record submittal.Call: None givenPlumage: first year female
